FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class TabView

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI TabView (controls\dev\TabView): a horizontal strip of tab headers atop the selected tab's content. The control OWNS its live tab collection (the WinUI TabItems vector, TabView.idl:135), seeded once from Items/Tabs at mount: the close button / middle-click / Ctrl+F4 remove the tab (after raising OnTabCloseRequested) with WinUI's reselect-at-the-removed-index rule (TabView.cpp:786-812); the add button appends what OnAddTabButtonClick returns; pointer drag reorders tabs (CanReorderTabs, default true). Equal width mode clamps tabs to 100–240px of the measured strip; overflowing tabs scroll behind 32×24 repeat scroll buttons (±50px, disabled at the extremes) and the selected tab is auto-scrolled into view. The selected header uses the SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary surface and fuses flush with the content area (the strip's 1px CardStroke baseline is per-tab and omitted under the selected header). Per-part restyling goes through Parts (see TemplateParts for the contract).

public sealed class TabView : Component

Fields

CanReorderTabs

WinUI CanReorderTabs (TabView.idl:142-143, default TRUE) — pointer drag reorders the strip.

public bool CanReorderTabs

Field Value

bool

CloseButtonOverlayMode

WinUI CloseButtonOverlayMode (TabView.idl:109-111, default Auto).

public TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode CloseButtonOverlayMode

Field Value

TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode

IsAddTabButtonVisible

WinUI IsAddTabButtonVisible (TabView.idl:119-120, default true).

public bool IsAddTabButtonVisible

Field Value

bool

Items

Rich path: full per-tab models (header + icon + closability + content). Mount-only seed of the control-owned collection (the WinUI TabItems vector, TabView.idl:135).

public IReadOnlyList<TabViewItem>? Items

Field Value

IReadOnlyList<TabViewItem>

OnAddTabButtonClick

WinUI AddTabButtonClick (TabView.idl:128): return the tab to append (the WinUI handler's TabItems.Add) or null for no-op. Null handler = the button clicks to nothing, like an unhandled WinUI event.

public Func<TabViewItem?>? OnAddTabButtonClick

Field Value

Func<TabViewItem>

OnSelectionChanged

WinUI SelectionChanged (TabView.idl:169) with the new index.

public Action<int>? OnSelectionChanged

Field Value

Action<int>

OnTabCloseRequested

WinUI TabCloseRequested (TabView.idl:124), raised with the closing index BEFORE the control commits the removal. WinUI leaves removal to the handler; our collection is control-owned (the TabItems vector), so the control performs the WinUI handler's TabItems.RemoveAt itself after raising.

public Action<int>? OnTabCloseRequested

Field Value

Action<int>

OnTabsReordered

Raised after a drag reorder commits (from, to) — the TabItemsChanged move notification (TabView.idl:130) so the app can mirror its model.

public Action<int, int>? OnTabsReordered

Field Value

Action<int, int>

PartAddButton

The trailing "+" add button (WinUI AddButton). Owned: OnClick, Role, OnRealized (chained).

public const string PartAddButton = "AddButton"

Field Value

string

PartContent

The selected tab's content area. Owned: Children.

public const string PartContent = "Content"

Field Value

string

PartStrip

The header strip row hosting the leading inset + scrolling tabs + add button. Owned: Children.

public const string PartStrip = "Strip"

Field Value

string

PartTabCloseButton

Each tab's trailing close button (WinUI TabViewItem CloseButton). Owned: OnClick (close), Role.

public const string PartTabCloseButton = "TabCloseButton"

Field Value

string

PartTabItem

Each tab's clickable header plate (WinUI TabViewItem TabContainer). The SAME modifier runs for every tab — branch on app state for per-tab styling. Owned: OnClick (select), Role, Children.

public const string PartTabItem = "TabItem"

Field Value

string

PartTabLabel

Each tab's header label — a TextEl, so style it via the generic map: Parts.Set<TextEl>(TabView.PartTabLabel, t => t with { … }). Owned: nothing (pure styling).

public const string PartTabLabel = "TabLabel"

Field Value

string

Parts

Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts; see TemplateParts for the contract.

public TemplateParts? Parts

Field Value

TemplateParts

SelectedIndex

Controlled selection: a two-way signal (the engine's controlled-prop idiom — plain fields are mount-only). Null = the control owns selection (WinUI SelectedIndex DP, TabView.idl:159-161).

public Signal<int>? SelectedIndex

Field Value

Signal<int>

TabWidthMode

WinUI TabWidthMode (TabView.idl:105-107, default Equal).

public TabViewWidthMode TabWidthMode

Field Value

TabViewWidthMode

Tabs

Simple path: header strings only (each becomes a closable TabViewItem). Mount-only seed of the control-owned collection; ignored when Items is set.

public IReadOnlyList<string> Tabs

Field Value

IReadOnlyList<string>

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
